channel.title: Retirement on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Retirement on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Reserved Virtual Machines Instances for select VM series
item.description:
Azure will retire certain Reserved VM Instance options for select VM series starting July 1, 2026. One-year purchases and renewals will stop for listed series, and the notice also indicates broader retirement of one-year and three-year reserved instance offerings for those series.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Prompt Flow in Azure ML and Foundry
item.description:
Prompt Flow in Microsoft Foundry and Azure Machine Learning will be retired on April 20, 2027 and replaced by Microsoft Agent Framework. The notice says the replacement is a single orchestration platform with new capabilities, but the provided excerpt does not include migration details.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Functions runtime v3 on Linux Consumption will stop running September 30, 2026
item.description:
Azure will enforce the retirement of Azure Functions runtime v3 for Linux Consumption–based Function Apps. The runtime was already retired on December 13, 2022, and affected apps will stop running on September 30, 2026.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Kubernetes Service support for Ubuntu 22.04
item.description:
Azure Kubernetes Service will retire support for Ubuntu 22.04 on June 30, 2027. Users should migrate to Ubuntu 24.04 or later before that date to avoid service disruption.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: End of lift reminder of HBv2/HC-Series/NP-Series Azure Virtual Machine in Azure Batch Pool
item.description:
Azure Batch will retire support for HBv2-series, HC-series, and NP-series VMs in Batch pools on May 31, 2027. The notice applies to these VM sizes only when used in Azure Batch pools.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Managed Grafana Essential SKU will retire on March 30, 2027
item.description:
Azure Managed Grafana Essential SKU will retire on March 30, 2027. Customers are advised to move to Azure Managed Grafana Standard SKU or Azure Monitor Dashboards with Grafana before that date to avoid service disruption.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Python support for Azure App Service and Azure Functions on Windows will be retired on March 31, 2027
item.description:
Python apps hosted on Azure App Service on Windows and Azure Functions on Windows will be retired on March 31, 2027. After that date, app configuration and content remain, but the apps will stop running.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: AzureDnsEndpoints for Azure storage accounts will be retired March 2027
item.description:
AzureDnsZone endpoints (preview) for Azure Blob storage accounts will be retired in March 2027. Microsoft advises moving all new and existing blob storage account deployments to Standard endpoints.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Service Fabric support for Windows Server 2022 ends on March 31, 2027 - upgrade to Windows Server 2025
item.description:
Service Fabric support for clusters running on Windows Server 2022 ends on March 31, 2027. Customers need to move to Windows Server 2025 before then to stay supported.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Service Fabric support for Windows Server 2019 ends on March 31, 2027 - upgrade to Windows Server 2025
item.description:
Service Fabric support for clusters running on Windows Server 2019 ends on March 31, 2027. Customers need to upgrade to Windows Server 2025 by then to stay supported.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Select Azure AI Language Features
item.description:
Microsoft will retire eight Azure AI Language features on March 20, 2027. The notice lists Key Phrase Extraction, Sentiment Analysis and Opinion Mining, Custom Text Classification, Conversational Language Understanding (CLU), Custom Question Answering (CQA), and other related language features.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: External Data Import & Data Connections in Azure Machine Learning will be retired on September 30, 2026
item.description:
Azure Machine Learning is retiring the Preview features for importing data from external sources and using external Data Connections. These features will stop being available after September 30, 2026.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Manually registered Azure VPN Clients for ​Azure Government and ​Microsoft Azure operated by 21Vianet clouds will be retired on March 31, 2029
item.description:
Manually registered Azure VPN clients for point-to-site connections using Microsoft Entra ID authentication will be retired on March 31, 2029 in Azure Government and Microsoft Azure operated by 21Vianet clouds. Users should take action to avoid disruption.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Cosmos DB for PostgreSQL will retire on March 31, 2029
item.description:
Azure Cosmos DB for PostgreSQL will be retired on March 31, 2029. Users must migrate to Azure Database for PostgreSQL Elastic Cluster before that date.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure SQL Elastic query - Shard_Map_Manager mode
item.description:
Azure SQL Elastic Query using the Shard_Map_Manager external data source type reaches end of support on March 31, 2027. Existing workloads can keep running, but this mode will no longer receive updates or support.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Sphere will be retired on July 31, 2031
item.description:
Azure Sphere will be retired on July 31, 2031. After that date, Microsoft will stop providing customer application, OS, bug, and security updates, as well as DAA certificate issuance.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Emissions Impact Dashboard for Azure
item.description:
The Emissions Impact Dashboard for Azure, hosted by Power BI, will be retired on March 31, 2027. After that date, it will no longer be accessible and technical support will end.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure VMware Solution AV36P and AV52 node retirement on June 30, 2029
item.description:
Microsoft will retire Azure VMware Solution AV36P and AV52 nodes on June 30, 2029. Existing AV36P and AV52 Reserved Instance terms are not affected, but customers should review RI expirations and plan migration before the retirement date.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Migration of Azure Batch Low-Priority VMs to Spot VMs in early March
item.description:
Azure Batch will begin a system-initiated migration from Low-Priority VMs to Spot VMs starting March 1, 2026. Low-Priority VMs were retired on September 30, 2025, and the change aligns Batch with Azure Spot infrastructure.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Support for Windows Server 2016 Marketplace images on Azure Batch pools will end on January 12, 2027
item.description:
Azure Batch will stop supporting Windows Server 2016 Marketplace images on Batch pools on January 12, 2027. This follows the end of extended support for Windows Server 2016.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: End of life reminder of NVv3 and NVv4-series Azure virtual machine in Azure Batch pools
item.description:
Microsoft Azure will end support for NVv3 and NVv4-series virtual machines in Azure Batch pools on September 30, 2026. The notice lists affected NVv3 sizes, including Standard_NV12s_v3, NV12hs_v3, NV24s_v3, NV24ms_v3, NV32ms_v3, and NV48s_v3.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Flatcar Container Linux for AKS (preview)
item.description:
Azure Kubernetes Service support for Flatcar Container Linux for AKS in preview is being retired on June 8, 2026. Users should move to a supported alternative by that date; the preview remains usable until June 7, 2026.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Policy faster enforcement and retirement of login/logout workaround
item.description:
Azure Policy now enforces new and updated Resource Manager mode policy assignments within 5 minutes. As a result, the previously recommended login/logout workaround (used to refresh cached policy state) is being retired.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Managed NGINX Ingress with Application Routing Add-on Retiring November 2026
item.description:
Microsoft will retire the Managed NGINX Ingress used by the AKS Application Routing add-on in November 2026 due to the upstream Ingress-NGINX project deprecating and ending updates after March 2026. Microsoft will provide only critical security patches until 30 Nov 2026, after which the controller is unsupported.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Windows Server Annual Channel (preview)
item.description:
Windows Server Annual Channel (Preview) on AKS will retire on May 15, 2026. Users should migrate to Windows Server Long Term Servicing Channel (LTSC) before that date.

  • Source: Azure Updates
